Some studies suggest that Ornithopteris esculenta may help alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions, such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. constituents: Ornithopteris esculenta, also known as the edible fern, contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The flavonoids present in Ornithopteris esculenta may also help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. preparations: Ornithopteris esculenta, also known as the tara vine, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried and made into a tea or infused in hot water to create a medicinal drink. The tara vine is rich in flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
